name: Partior

issuer.name: Partior Pte Ltd (JV: DBS, JPMorgan, Standard Chartered, Temasek)

custodian.name: Member banks act as custodians for own tokenized deposits

regulator.name: Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS); part of Project Guardian

summary: Singapore-based interbank wholesale settlement network for tokenized commercial bank deposits across multiple currencies. Founded by DBS, JPMorgan, and Temasek; expanded to include Standard Chartered, Deutsche Bank, iFAST. Enables atomic 24/7 cross-border settlement in USD, SGD, EUR. Settled live transactions since 2021; processed several billion USD cumulatively. Most operationally mature institutional tokenized payment infrastructure in Asia; precursor architecture for what MAS Project Guardian aims to scale.

sources.title: MAS: Partior featured in Project Guardian reports

underlying_asset: Commercial bank deposit liabilities at member banks; not tokenized central bank money. Each bank issues its own tokenized version of its deposit liability.
reserve_composition: 1:1 with corresponding bank deposit at issuing bank; redeemable on-network only between member banks.
secondary_market: Permissioned network — only member banks can hold and transfer tokens; not publicly tradable.
oracle_provider: N/A — atomic on-chain settlement between bank ledgers.
redemption_mechanism: Atomic redemption against issuing bank's reserve at issuing bank.
upgrade_authority: Partior Pte Ltd board (representing JV partners); changes require consortium consensus.

timeline.event: Standard Chartered joins as strategic investor
Deutsche Bank, iFAST integration; multi-currency support expanded
Featured in MAS Project Guardian Phase 2 industry pilots
